attractions.name: American Coney Island (American Coney Island Official Website)
attractions.description: Established in 1917, American Coney Island is one of Detroit's oldest family-owned businesses, famous for its signature Coney hot dogs and legendary rivalry with its neighbor. The restaurant features a classic diner atmosphere and continues to use the original Keros family secret chili recipe. (American Coney Island Official Website)
attractions.address: 114 W Lafayette Blvd, Detroit, MI 48226, USA (American Coney Island Official Website)
attractions.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM - 7:00 PM; Tuesday: 10:00 AM - 7:00 PM; Wednesday: 10:00 AM - 7:00 PM; Thursday: 10:00 AM - 7:00 PM; Friday: 10:00 AM - 12:00 AM; Saturday: 11:00 AM - 12:00 AM; Sunday: 11:00 AM - 7:00 PM (Visit Detroit)
attractions.admission: Free (Restaurant; Menu prices apply) (SinglePlatform Menu)
highlights: Founder Constantine "Gust" Keros opened the restaurant in 1917 after immigrating from Greece. (American Coney Island Official Website)
The signature Coney Dog uses specially-seasoned Dearborn Sausage hot dogs. (The D Las Vegas)
Iconic "Coney Challenge" rivalry with neighbor Lafayette Coney Island. (YouFoundSarah)
Features a secret family recipe for its savory meat chili sauce. (The D Las Vegas)
Has been featured on TV shows like Man v. Food and Food Wars. (Wikipedia))
Open 24 hours at the Las Vegas location and traditionally 24 hours in Detroit, serving as a late-night staple. (The D Las Vegas)
attractions.tips: Try the "Coney Challenge" by tasting hot dogs from both American and Lafayette next door to decide your favorite. Bring cash for a quicker experience, although cards are accepted. (YouFoundSarah, Wanderlustingk)
attractions.nearby: Lafayette Coney Island, Campus Martius Park, Guardian Building, Spirit of Detroit Plaza (Lafayette Coney Island)

attractions.name: Book Tower (Book Tower and Book Building)
attractions.description: The Book Tower is a 38-story historic skyscraper in downtown Detroit that serves as a cornerstone of the Washington Boulevard Historic District. Originally completed in 1926 by architect Louis Kamper, it underwent a seven-year, $300 million restoration to become a premier mixed-use destination featuring luxury residences, the ROOST Apartment Hotel, and world-class dining. Book Tower Official Site, Wikipedia
attractions.region: Downtown Detroit (Washington Boulevard Historic District) Wikipedia, Zillow
attractions.hours: General Public Access: Daily, typically 9:00 AM – 11:00 PM (varies by venue); Business/Office: Monday – Friday 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Security Desk: 24/7 Book Tower Residences FAQ, ApartmentGuide.com
attractions.days_open: Daily (Public areas and dining); Monday – Friday (Business/Office) ApartmentGuide.com, Book Tower Residences
attractions.admission: Free (General admission to public lobby, common areas, and historical exhibition) Airial Travel, Viator
highlights: The 3-story glass rotunda crown jewel with 6,000 glass panels and 7,000 jewel embellishments Book Tower Official Site
29 stone caryatids (female sculptures) that adorn the building's façade and roof cornice Book Tower Official Site
Kamper's Rooftop Bar on the 14th floor offering sweeping views of the Detroit skyline Book Tower Official Site
The Historic Exhibition featuring a collection of artifacts gathered by the Detroit Historical Society Book Tower Official Site
Restored Italian Renaissance and Neoclassical interiors, including original plaster ceilings and bronze elevator doors Hour Detroit Magazine
attractions.tips: The ground floor lobby is open to the public and well-worth a walkthrough to see the restored rotunda; Kamper's Rooftop Bar and Le Suprême often require reservations during peak times. Reddit /r/Detroit, Airial Travel
attractions.nearby: Campus Martius Park, Grand Circus Park, The BELT (artistic alley), Detroit Riverwalk, and the Westin Book Cadillac Hotel Visit Detroit, Book Tower Official Site

attractions.name: Cadillac Square (Downtown Detroit Partnership)
attractions.description: Once the site of the Detroit Central Market, Cadillac Square is a re-established public park located in the heart of Downtown Detroit, immediately east of Campus Martius Park. The space serves as a vibrant community hub, featuring the historic Bagley Memorial Fountain, perennial gardens, and a variety of year-round seating. It is well-known for hosting seasonal events such as the Cadillac Lodge, food truck rallies like Downtown Street Eats, and local markets. (Downtown Detroit Partnership)
attractions.address: 662 Woodward Ave, Detroit, MI 48226 (The Detroit Photography Architecture Guide)
attractions.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 6:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 6:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Thursday: 6:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Friday: 6:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Saturday: 6:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Sunday: 6:00 AM - 10:00 PM (Waze)
attractions.admission: Free (Downtown Detroit Partnership)
highlights: Bagley Memorial Fountain, the city's only remaining structure by architect Henry Hobson Richardson (Wikipedia)
Downtown Street Eats, Michigan's largest weekly food truck rally (Downtown Detroit Partnership)
Thursday Markets featuring local vendors and small businesses (Downtown Detroit Partnership)
The Cadillac Lodge, a seasonal holiday destination with cozy indoor seating (Decked Out Detroit)
Beautifully maintained perennial gardens and ample public seating (Downtown Detroit Partnership)
A central location surrounded by iconic Detroit skyscrapers like Cadillac Tower (Historic Detroit)
attractions.tips: Visit during weekday lunch hours (11:00 AM – 2:00 PM) to catch the Downtown Street Eats food truck rally. During the winter months, the park transforms into a holiday destination with cozy seating at the Cadillac Lodge and a local Christmas market. (Downtown Detroit Partnership)
attractions.nearby: Campus Martius Park, Cadillac Tower, Monroe Street Midway, First National Building, Detroit Riverfront (Downtown Detroit Partnership)

attractions.name: Campus Martius Park
attractions.description: Campus Martius Park is the signature public space in the heart of Downtown Detroit, often referred to as "Detroit's Gathering Place." Named the No. 1 "Top Public Square" in the U.S. by USA Today, it was re-established in 2004 at the site of the city's point of origin. The park serves as a year-round destination, hosting major events and featuring seasonal highlights like an ice rink in winter and an urban beach in summer. Downtown Detroit Partnership, Visit Detroit
attractions.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Tuesday: 7:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Wednesday: 7:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Thursday: 7:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Friday: 7:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Saturday: 7:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Sunday: 7:00 AM - 11:00 PM Visit Detroit
attractions.admission: Free; The Rink (Seasonal): $12 Adults (13+), $9 Children (12 & under), $6 Skate Rental Downtown Detroit Partnership, LittleGuide Detroit
highlights: The Rink at Campus Martius Park: A premier outdoor ice skating experience from November to March.
The Beach: A sun-soaked urban oasis with sand and lounge chairs, open May through October.
Annual Tree Lighting: A major holiday event featuring Detroit's official Christmas tree.
Woodward Fountain: A majestic water feature at the center of the park.
Michigan Soldiers and Sailors Monument: A historic Civil War memorial located within the park.
Live Entertainment and Movie Nights: Free seasonal programming including concerts and outdoor films.
attractions.tips: Public parking is available in the garage under One Kennedy Square or the nearby Z Parking Garage, which features unique murals. Most park programming, including concerts and movies, is free to the public. Downtown Detroit Partnership, Parc Detroit
attractions.nearby: Cadillac Square, The Guardian Building, Detroit People Mover, Monroe Street Midway, Penobscot Building Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Comerica Park (MLB.com)
attractions.description: Comerica Park is a premier open-air ballpark in downtown Detroit that has served as the home of the MLB's Detroit Tigers since 2000 (Wikipedia). It is celebrated for its distinctive "retro-classic" architecture, offering fans spectacular views of the Detroit skyline and a variety of family-friendly attractions (Detroit Sports Commission). The venue features unique amenities such as a carousel, a Ferris wheel, and a decade-by-decade pedestrian museum known as the Tigers Walk of Fame (MLB.com).
attractions.hours: Regular Box Office: Monday – Friday: 11:00 a.m. – 6:00 p.m.; Closed on Saturdays & Sundays (Non-Game Days). Game Days: 11:00 a.m. – End of Game (10:00 a.m. for 1:10 p.m. starts) (313 Presents).
attractions.days_open: Open daily during the MLB season and for scheduled events (MLB.com).
attractions.admission: Game tickets typically range from $2 to $100 for standard seating, with premium VIP options up to $9,000 (TicketSmarter). Public tours are $25 per person (MLB.com). Amusement rides like the carousel and Ferris wheel are $2 per person, though they are free for children 14 and under on Sundays (MLB.com).
highlights: A 15-foot massive tiger statue at the main entrance (Wikipedia).
The Hi-Chew Carousel featuring 30 hand-painted tigers (MLB.com).
Fly Ball Ferris Wheel with cars shaped like baseballs (MLB.com).
Tigers Walk of Fame, a historical concourse museum (Encyclopedia of Detroit).
Heroic-sized statues of legendary players Ty Cobb, Al Kaline, and others (Wikipedia).
The Chevrolet Fountain in center field that "roars" with liquid fireworks (Wikipedia).
attractions.tips: Comerica Park is a cashless venue, so ensure you have credit or mobile payment options (MLB.com). For families, Sunday games are 'Kids Days' featuring free carousel and Ferris wheel rides for children (Visit Detroit). Arrive at least two hours early if you want to explore the Walk of Fame and statues without the crowds (MLB.com).
attractions.nearby: Ford Field, Fox Theatre, Campus Martius Park, Detroit RiverWalk, and the Motown Museum (Tripadvisor).

attractions.name: Detroit Beer Company
attractions.description: The Detroit Beer Company is a well-known brewpub located in the historic Broadway Avenue Historic District of downtown Detroit. Established in 2003, it features a custom ten-hectoliter brewhouse and serves a rotating selection of house-made beers along with a menu of eclectic American cuisine. It is a popular destination for pre- and post-game dining due to its proximity to major sports and entertainment venues. (Official Site, Visit Detroit)
attractions.region: Downtown Detroit / Broadway Avenue Historic District (Official Site, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed (Open for events/games); Tuesday - Thursday: 11:00 am - 11:00 pm; Friday - Saturday: 11:00 am - 12:00 am; Sunday: 12:00 pm - 10:00 pm (Official Site)
attractions.days_open: Tuesday - Sunday (Monday for special events) (Official Site)
attractions.admission: Free (Yelp)
highlights: Housed in the historic 100-year-old Hartz Building in the Broadway Avenue Historic District. (Official Site)
Offers 7-9 fresh house-made brews daily with a rotating lineup. (Official Site)
Features two floors of seating with views of the custom-fabricated brewhouse. (Official Site)
Menu includes diverse American options such as pizzas, pastas, and unique sandwiches. (Official Site)
Known as the first modern brewpub in downtown Detroit. (Yelp)
Prime location just steps away from Comerica Park and Ford Field. (Visit Detroit)
attractions.tips: Visit during Happy Hour (Monday-Friday, typically 4-6 PM) for deals on beers and appetizers. It is a great spot to grab a drink before Detroit Tigers or Lions games, but it can get very crowded, so consider arriving early. Reservations are available for large parties and special events. (Yelp, Official Site)
attractions.nearby: Comerica Park, Ford Field, Detroit Opera House, Grand Circus Park, Gem Theatre, Music Hall Center for the Performing Arts (Official Site, Google Maps)

attractions.name: Detroit International Riverfront
attractions.description: The Detroit International Riverfront is a prominent 5.5-mile tourist attraction and landmark extending from the Ambassador Bridge to Belle Isle. It features a continuous Riverwalk, multiple parks, greenways, and a cruise ship terminal, offering a safe and scenic venue for recreation, festivals, and public art. Managed by the Detroit Riverfront Conservancy, it has been repeatedly voted the best riverwalk in the United States.
attractions.address: 600 Renaissance Center, Suite 1720 (Conservancy HQ); Main access points at 1340 Atwater St (Cullen Plaza) and 1801 W. Jefferson (Ralph Wilson Park)
attractions.hours: 6:00 AM - 10:00 PM daily (Detroit Riverfront Conservancy). Some specific areas like the Delta Dental Play Garden (closes 9:00 PM) or seasonal attractions have modified hours.
attractions.admission: Free (Detroit Riverfront Conservancy). Specific amenities like the Cullen Family Carousel are also free during seasonal operation (LittleGuide Detroit). Commercial tours like Diamond Jack's River Tours require paid tickets (Airial Travel).
highlights: Cullen Family Carousel - A custom-designed carousel featuring local wildlife (Detroit Riverfront Conservancy).
Dequindre Cut Greenway - A 2-mile urban trail known for its vibrant public graffiti art (Wikipedia).
Milliken State Park & Harbor - Michigan's first urban state park featuring a 63-foot lighthouse (Wikipedia).
Ralph C. Wilson, Jr. Centennial Park - A 22-acre regional park with large-animal themed play structures and a water garden (Detroit Riverfront Conservancy).
Robert C. Valade Park - Features a sandy beach, children's play area, and seasonal sledding hill (Detroit Riverfront Conservancy).
Mt. Elliott Park & Splash Pad - A popular water-themed park for families (Detroit Riverfront Conservancy).
attractions.tips: Free parking is available in associated lots on a first-come, first-served basis; children under 18 must be accompanied by an adult after 6 PM (Detroit Riverfront Conservancy). Bike rentals are available at Cullen Plaza through MoGo (Detroit Riverfront Conservancy).
attractions.nearby: Renaissance Center (GM World), Hart Plaza, Belle Isle Park, Eastern Market (via Dequindre Cut), and the Aretha Franklin Amphitheater (Wikipedia).

attractions.name: Detroit People Mover (DPM)
attractions.description: The Detroit People Mover is a 2.94-mile elevated automated transit system that operates in a one-way loop encircling downtown Detroit. It connects 13 stations at key locations including major sporting venues, entertainment districts, and international landmarks, providing a scenic and efficient way to navigate the city's central business district. Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Monday-Friday: 6:30 AM – 12:00 AM; Saturday: 10:00 AM – 12:00 AM; Sunday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM Official Website
attractions.admission: Free (Zero-fare pilot program sponsored by Priority Waste through at least 2025) Official Website, Wikipedia
highlights: 13 uniquely designed stations, each featuring significant original works of public art. Official Website
Fully automated, driverless technology operating on a single-track elevated loop. Wikipedia
Zero-fare service for all passengers through a corporate-sponsored pilot program. Official Website
Direct access to major Detroit landmarks like Huntington Place, the Renaissance Center, and Greektown. Visit Detroit
Convenient connections to the QLine streetcar and regional bus systems for broader city access. Wikipedia
attractions.tips: Check for extended hours during major downtown events like the Detroit Grand Prix or music festivals. Use the system as a cost-effective alternative to downtown parking when visiting theaters or stadiums. Take time to view the unique art installations at each station, which include mosaics, sculptures, and murals. Note that some station facility entrances (like Huntington Place) may close earlier than the transit service itself. Official Website
attractions.nearby: Huntington Place, Renaissance Center, Little Caesars Arena, Comerica Park, Ford Field, Greektown, Campus Martius Park, Detroit Riverwalk, Grand Circus Park Official Website

attractions.name: Detroit–Windsor International Freedom Festival (Ford Fireworks)
attractions.description: The Detroit-Windsor International Freedom Festival is a premier multi-day celebration marking Canada Day (July 1) and American Independence Day (July 4). The event features one of the world's largest and most spectacular fireworks displays over the Detroit River, drawing over a million spectators to the shores of both Detroit and Windsor (Wikipedia, Hour Detroit).
attractions.address: 1 Hart Plaza (Main Detroit Viewing Area), Detroit, MI 48226; Organized by The Parade Company, 9500 Mt. Elliott St, Studio A, Detroit, MI 48211
attractions.hours: Riverfront Parks: Open daily 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Ford Fireworks Event: Monday, June 22, 2026, typically starting at 9:55 PM; Shimmer on the River: Thursday, June 18, 2026, 5:00 PM – 9:00 PM (Detroit Riverfront Conservancy, Windsor Essex Festival Guide).
attractions.days_open: Late June annually (Main Fireworks scheduled for Monday, June 22, 2026)
attractions.admission: Free (General public viewing at Hart Plaza and along the RiverWalk); Ticketed options available for private parties such as the Ford Fireworks Rooftop Party ($350+) and Shimmer on the River ($150-$250) (The Parade Company, Detroit Riverfront Conservancy).
highlights: Massive 24-minute choreographed fireworks display over the Detroit River (Visit Detroit).
Live musical performances and family-oriented activities at Hart Plaza and Valade Park (Detroit Riverfront Conservancy).
Synchronized musical simulcast broadcast on WJR-AM and WDIV-TV Local 4 (The Parade Company).
Fundraising events like "Shimmer on the River" featuring local Detroit food and interactive art (Detroit Riverfront Conservancy).
Carnival rides, games, and midway concessions on the Windsor, Ontario side of the riverfront (Wikipedia).
attractions.tips: Arrive at the riverfront several hours early to secure a viewing spot, as areas like Hart Plaza fill up quickly. Consider using public transportation or the free "Transit Windsor" shuttles to avoid significant traffic congestion and high parking fees (Wikipedia).
attractions.nearby: GM Renaissance Center, Detroit RiverWalk, Belle Isle Park, Aretha Franklin Amphitheatre, William G. Milliken State Park (Pure Michigan).

attractions.name: Ford Field Official Site
attractions.description: Ford Field is a premier multi-purpose indoor stadium located in the heart of Downtown Detroit and serves as the official home of the NFL's Detroit Lions. Opened in 2002, the venue is notable for its unique architectural integration of the historic Hudson's warehouse and features a large glass wall that provides spectacular views of the city skyline. Official Site, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 2000 Brush St., Detroit, MI 48226 Official Site
attractions.region: Downtown Detroit / Stadium District Official Site, Yelp
attractions.hours: Ticket Office: Mon-Fri 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Event Days: Variable (typically gates open 2-2.5 hours before kickoff) Official Site, Visit Detroit
attractions.admission: Varies by event; Stadium Tours (when available): Adult $7, Seniors $5, Children $5 (under 5 Free) Fly To The Game
highlights: Home stadium of the NFL's Detroit Lions Official Site
Unique design incorporating the historic Hudson's warehouse Wikipedia
Host of major events including Super Bowl XL and the NCAA Final Four Wikipedia
Expansive glass windows offering views of the Detroit skyline Official Site
State-of-the-art 2017 renovation for enhanced fan experience Official Site
attractions.tips: Follow the NFL's Clear Bag Policy; arrive early for 'Power Hour' specials; use the Detroit People Mover for transit. Official Site, Visit Detroit
attractions.nearby: Comerica Park, Greektown, Detroit Opera House, Music Hall Center for the Performing Arts Pure Michigan

attractions.name: Fox Theatre
attractions.description: Opened in 1928, the Fox Theatre is a National Historic Landmark and one of the most iconic music venues in the world. Designed by architect C. Howard Crane in a "Siamese Byzantine" style, it remains the largest surviving movie palace from the 1920s and serves as a premier destination for Broadway musicals, concerts, and comedy shows (313 Presents, Wikipedia))
attractions.hours: Open on event days only: typically 12:00 PM – Event start time (Box Office opens 60 minutes before morning events) (313 Presents, Ticketmaster)
attractions.days_open: Open on event days only (313 Presents, Ticketmaster)
attractions.admission: Prices vary by event; Guided tours are $25 per person (313 Presents, Box Office Ticket Sales)
highlights: Stunning "Siamese Byzantine" architectural design featuring Egyptian, Hindu, and Buddhist influences (313 Presents)
Largest surviving movie palace of the 1920s with a seating capacity of over 5,000 (Wikipedia))
Home to the second largest Wurlitzer organ in the United States (Experience Detroit)
Original 1928 hand-operated elevators that are still in use today (313 Presents)
Meticulously restored to its original 1920s grandeur in 1988 (Wikipedia))
attractions.tips: The venue is cashless; only credit/debit cards are accepted for concessions and merchandise. Bags larger than 4" x 6" x 1.5" are strictly prohibited, except for medical and diaper bags. It is highly recommended to book parking in advance via services like ParkWhiz (313 Presents, Ticketmaster)
attractions.nearby: Comerica Park, Ford Field, Little Caesars Arena, The Fillmore Detroit, Grand Circus Park, Hockeytown Cafe (Tripadvisor, Yelp)

attractions.name: Grand Circus Park Historic District
attractions.description: Grand Circus Park is a 5-acre historic urban oasis that anchors Detroit's entertainment and residential districts. Bisected by Woodward Avenue, the park features beautiful historic fountains, lush green spaces, and a popular dog park. It serves as a central gathering place for residents and visitors alike, hosting numerous free community events throughout the year. (Downtown Detroit Partnership)
attractions.hours: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM Daily (Waze, Wanderlog)
attractions.admission: Free (Downtown Detroit Partnership)
highlights: Russell A. Alger Memorial Fountain, a work by sculptor Daniel Chester French (Wikipedia)
Grand Circus Dog Park, a popular off-leash area for local pets (Downtown Detroit Partnership)
Statues of Michigan governors Hazen Pingree and William Maybury (Wikipedia)
Historic architecture surroundings including the David Whitney and David Broderick Tower (Detroit1701.org)
Thomas Edison Memorial Fountain located in the western half of the park (Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: Visitors can easily access the park via the Detroit People Mover's Grand Circus Park station or the QLine. The park is a great spot for people-watching and enjoying a lunch outdoors, and it's particularly lively during Tigers game days at nearby Comerica Park. (Airial Travel)
attractions.nearby: Comerica Park, Ford Field, Fox Theatre, Detroit Opera House, Little Caesars Arena, Fillmore Detroit (Downtown Detroit Partnership)

attractions.name: Greater Penobscot Building
attractions.description: The Penobscot Building is an iconic 47-story Art Deco skyscraper located in the heart of the Detroit Financial District. Completed in 1928 and designed by architect Wirt C. Rowland, it was the tallest building in Michigan until 1977 and remains a prominent symbol of the city's architectural heritage. (Wikipedia, Official Website)
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Tuesday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Thursday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Friday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Saturday: Closed, Sunday: Closed (Apple Maps, Airial Travel)
attractions.admission: Free (Access to the lobby and exterior is free; upper floors are restricted to tenants) (Airial Travel)
highlights: Art Deco masterpiece designed by Wirt C. Rowland in 1928 (Historic Detroit)
Distinctive red neon beacon tower that dominates the downtown skyline (Penobscot Building)
Ornate lobby featuring marble work and Native American motifs by Corrado Parducci (Wikipedia)
Innovative 'H' shape floor plan designed to maximize natural light for occupants (Airial Travel)
Contributing property in the Detroit Financial Historic District and on the National Register of Historic Places (National Park Service)
attractions.tips: Respect building security as photography inside can be strictly regulated; admire the grand architecture from street level at Campus Martius Park for the best views; stop by Detroit Donuts on the ground floor for coffee. (Airial Travel)
attractions.nearby: Guardian Building, Campus Martius Park, Buhl Building, One Woodward Avenue, Detroit Riverwalk (Airial Travel, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Greektown Historic District (Greektown Detroit)
attractions.category: Entertainment and Cultural District, Historic District
attractions.description: Greektown Detroit is a celebrated entertainment and cultural district and the traditional center of Detroit’s Greek community. It is home to one of the last surviving Victorian-era commercial streetscapes in downtown Detroit, featuring vibrant dining, nightlife, and historic architecture. (Greektown Neighborhood Partnership, Visit Detroit)
attractions.address: 400 Monroe Street, Suite 340 (District Office); Monroe Street (Main Corridor)
attractions.hours: Mon: Open 24 hours, Tue: Open 24 hours, Wed: Open 24 hours, Thu: Open 24 hours, Fri: Open 24 hours, Sat: Open 24 hours, Sun: Open 24 hours (District access; business hours vary)
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Pegasus Taverna - Iconic Greek dining experience known for authentic cuisine and Saganaki. (Greektown Directory)
Hollywood Casino at Greektown - A major gaming and entertainment destination featuring a hotel and diverse dining options. (Hollywood Casino)
Astoria Pastry Shop - A historic bakery famous for its Greek and European sweets. (Greektown Directory)
Detroit People Mover (Greektown Station) - Provides easy elevated transit access directly into the heart of the district. (Detroit People Mover)
Old St. Mary’s Catholic Church - A beautiful historic landmark within the district. (Greektown Directory)
Greektown at Sundown - Seasonal weekend events where Monroe Street becomes a pedestrian-only zone with music and dance. (Expedia)
attractions.tips: Ride the Detroit People Mover to the Greektown Station for the most convenient arrival. Many visitors utilize the Hollywood Casino garage for parking, though policies may vary during major events. The district is best explored on foot to enjoy the neon signs and historic architecture. (Visit Detroit, Greektown Neighborhood Partnership)
attractions.nearby: Campus Martius Park, Comerica Park, Ford Field, Detroit Opera House, Renaissance Center, Cadillac Square. (Bedrock Detroit)

attractions.name: Huntington Place (formerly Cobo Hall/Cobo Center) (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: Huntington Place is a world-class convention center located in downtown Detroit along the scenic Detroit International Riverfront. As the 16th largest convention center in the United States, it features over 723,000 square feet of exhibition space and is the longtime home of the North American International Auto Show (Wikipedia, Official Website).
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily for building access; event hours vary by schedule (Waze, Yelp)
attractions.admission: Free to enter the building; individual event ticket prices vary by show (Vivid Seats, TicketNetwork)
highlights: Home to the North American International Auto Show since 1965 (Wikipedia)
Features the 40,000 sq. ft. Grand Riverview Ballroom with floor-to-ceiling glass (Official Website)
A massive 2,400,000-square-foot facility with 98 meeting rooms (Wikipedia)
Connected to the Detroit People Mover with its own dedicated station (Wikipedia)
Integrated with the award-winning Detroit RiverWalk (Official Website)
Managed by the Detroit Regional Convention Facility Authority (DRCFA) (Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: Utilize the 'Show Your Badge' program for discounts at local businesses. Use the Detroit People Mover for free, convenient transport directly into the venue (Official Website).
attractions.nearby: Detroit RiverWalk, Hart Plaza, Guardian Building, Renaissance Center, Campus Martius Park (Official Website, Visit Detroit)

attractions.name: Jacoby's German Biergarten (Jacoby's Bar) (Official Website)
attractions.description: Jacoby’s Bar stands as Detroit’s oldest biergarten, serving traditional German flavors and craft brews since the late 1800s. Nestled in the historic Bricktown neighborhood, it offers a rustic, old-world atmosphere with original woodwork and tin ceilings that transport visitors back to the city's heyday. (Official Website, Michigan Enjoyer)
attractions.hours: Monday: 12:00 PM - 2:00 AM, Tuesday: 12:00 PM - 2:00 AM, Wednesday: 12:00 PM - 2:00 AM, Thursday: 12:00 PM - 2:00 AM, Friday: 12:00 PM - 2:00 AM, Saturday: 12:00 PM - 2:00 AM, Sunday: 12:00 PM - 2:00 AM (Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Free (Tripadvisor)
highlights: Detroit’s oldest biergarten, established in 1904 (Official Website)
Authentic German cuisine including Kartoffelpuffer (potato pancakes) and Schnitzel (Official Website)
Extensive selection of classic German brews and rotating craft options (Official Website)
Located in the historic Bricktown district with charming original interior woodwork (Michigan Enjoyer)
Upstairs cocktail lounge called Room 1904 specializing in absinthe (Official Website)
Historic vertical sign and window boxes adorning the brick facade (Detroitdvotion)
attractions.tips: Try the signature Kartoffelpuffer served with chunky applesauce and sour cream, or the house-made Corned Beef Reuben. For a unique experience, visit the upstairs cocktail lounge, Room 1904, to explore a variety of absinthe offerings. (Hour Detroit Magazine, Official Website)
attractions.nearby: Bricktown Historic District, St. Andrews Hall, Campus Martius Park, Detroit RiverFront, The Guardian Building (Tripadvisor, Kids Kiddle)

attractions.name: Lafayette Coney Island
attractions.description: Established in 1924, Lafayette Coney Island is an iconic Detroit institution known for its authentic Detroit-style Coney dogs. Located in the heart of downtown, it offers a legendary old-school diner experience and a historic sibling rivalry with American Coney Island next door (Lafayette Coney Island, Wikipedia)).
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM - 3:00 AM, Tuesday: 9:00 AM - 3:00 AM, Wednesday: 9:00 AM - 3:00 AM, Thursday: 9:00 AM - 3:00 AM, Friday: 8:00 AM - 4:00 AM, Saturday: 8:00 AM - 4:00 AM, Sunday: 9:00 AM - 3:00 AM (Tripadvisor)
attractions.days_open: Open Seven Days a Week (Lafayette Coney Island)
attractions.admission: Free (Lafayette Coney Island)
highlights: Signature Detroit-style Coney dog with chili, onions, and mustard (Lafayette Coney Island)
Uses natural-casing beef hot dogs from local Dearborn Sausage (Lafayette Coney Island)
Historic sibling rivalry with American Coney Island, situated right next door (Wikipedia))
Authentic, bustling, and old-school Detroit diner atmosphere (Lafayette Coney Island)
Visited by notable figures including Joe Biden and Jennifer Granholm (Lafayette Coney Island)
attractions.tips: Choosing a side between Lafayette and its rival next door is considered a Detroit rite of passage (Lafayette Coney Island). While historically a cash-only business, the restaurant now accepts credit cards (YouTube). It is a popular destination before or after events at nearby stadiums like Ford Field or Comerica Park (Lafayette Coney Island).
attractions.nearby: Campus Martius Park, Guardian Building, Spirit of Detroit Plaza, Comerica Park, Little Caesars Arena, Ford Field (Lafayette Coney Island)

attractions.name: MGM Grand Detroit (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: MGM Grand Detroit is the first-ever Las Vegas-style destination to open in a major metropolitan core, featuring a massive 147,000-square-foot casino and a luxury 400-room hotel. The resort offers a premier gaming experience with thousands of slots and table games, alongside the only resort-style spa in Southeast Michigan and various high-end dining options (MGM Resorts, MGM Resorts Fact Sheet).
attractions.hours: Casino: Open 24 hours daily; Hotel Check-in: 4:00 PM, Check-out: 11:00 AM (MGM Resorts Fact Sheet, Travel Weekly)
attractions.admission: Free for casino entry (must be 21+); Hotel rates typically range from $179 to $499+ per night (Travel Weekly, Expedia)
highlights: 147,000-square-foot casino floor with over 2,800 slot machines and nearly 150 table games (MGM Resorts Fact Sheet)
IMMERSE Spa, the only resort-style spa in Southeast Michigan (Tripadvisor)
BetMGM Sportsbook featuring 60 HDTVs, betting windows, and self-service kiosks (MGM Resorts Fact Sheet)
Diverse dining options including D.PRIME Steakhouse and the Detroit Central Market food hall (MGM Resorts Fact Sheet)
Luxury accommodations with 400 guest rooms, including 65 opulent suites (Travel Weekly)
attractions.tips: All hotel and casino guests must be 21 years of age or older. For the best access from the airport, take I-94 East to M-10 South and exit at Bagley Avenue. Check the official website for seasonal facility closures, such as annual spa or pool maintenance (MGM Resorts, MGM Resorts Official Site).
attractions.nearby: Little Caesars Arena, Comerica Park, Ford Field, Campus Martius Park, and Huntington Place (MGM Resorts Fact Sheet, Expedia)

attractions.name: Michigan Theatre (Michigan Building)
attractions.category: Landmark, Historic Site, Parking Garage Historic Detroit
attractions.description: Opened in 1926 as a 4,000-seat French Renaissance movie palace, the Michigan Theatre was once one of Detroit's most opulent entertainment venues. Following its closure in 1976, the auditorium was uniquely converted into a three-story parking garage to serve the adjacent Michigan Building, leaving much of its ornate plasterwork and grand ceiling intact above the parked cars. Historic Detroit, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 238 Bagley Avenue, Detroit, MI 48226 Historic Detroit, Tripadvisor
attractions.region: Downtown Detroit / Grand Circus Park District Historic Detroit
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 8:00 AM - 4:00 PM (for visitors) Yelp
attractions.admission: Free for visitors; parking fees may apply on event days (approx. $10) Yelp
highlights: Architectural anomaly featuring a functioning parking garage inside a gutted 1920s movie palace.
Intricate French Renaissance plasterwork and a massive domed ceiling preserved above the parking decks.
Built on the historic site of Henry Ford's first workshop where he constructed the Quadricycle.
Designed by the famous theater architects Rapp & Rapp, who also designed the Chicago Theatre.
Formerly hosted legendary performers such as Frank Sinatra, Louis Armstrong, and the Marx Brothers.
attractions.tips: The best time to visit is during weekday business hours when the garage is accessible to the public. Be sure to look up to appreciate the stunning architectural details, and remember that it is a working parking facility, so be mindful of moving vehicles. Yelp, Atlas Obscura
attractions.nearby: Grand Circus Park, Fox Theatre, Detroit Opera House, Comerica Park Tripadvisor

attractions.name: MotorCity Casino Hotel
attractions.description: MotorCity Casino Hotel is Detroit's only locally-owned and operated luxury casino and entertainment complex. The $825 million facility features a 100,000-square-foot casino, a 400-room luxury hotel, a performance venue named Sound Board, and the historic Wagner Baking Company building. Wikipedia, Official Website
attractions.hours: 24/7 Visit Detroit
attractions.admission: Free (Admission to the casino is free; hotel rooms, spa treatments, and concert tickets have separate costs) Expedia
highlights: 100,000-square-foot casino with approximately 2,500 slot machines and 59 table games Wikipedia
Sound Board, a live music theater with a capacity of 2,400 people Wikipedia
D.Tour Spa, a 13,000-square-foot full-service spa with 10 treatment rooms Hotels.com
Multiple dining options including the AAA Four-Diamond Iridescence and Revel steakhouse Trivago, Hotels.com
Complimentary shuttle service to nearby sports and concert venues Trivago
Free self-parking and valet parking for guests Official Website
attractions.tips: Guests must be 21 or older to enter the casino. The hotel offers a 'Stay & Play' package which includes Reward Play. To-go breakfast is available for a fee each morning. Visit Detroit, Official Website, Hotels.com
attractions.nearby: Little Caesars Arena, Fox Theatre, Comerica Park, Ford Field, MGM Grand Detroit Casino Travelocity

attractions.name: Philip A. Hart Plaza (City of Detroit)
attractions.description: Philip A. Hart Plaza is a 14-acre urban plaza located along the Detroit River in downtown Detroit, which serves as a premier venue for outdoor cultural and musical events. Opened in 1975, it is situated on the historic site where Antoine de la Mothe Cadillac landed in 1701 to found the settlement that became Detroit. (City of Detroit, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: 1 Hart Plaza, Detroit, MI 48226 (City of Detroit)
attractions.hours: Mon-Sun: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Standard office/staff hours), Event hours vary (Evendo, City of Detroit)
attractions.admission: Free (Tripadvisor)
highlights: Horace E. Dodge and Son Memorial Fountain, a monumental stainless steel water feature designed by Isamu Noguchi (Wikipedia)
The Pylon, a 120-foot tall stainless steel spire sculpture (Wikipedia)
Gateway to Freedom International Memorial to the Underground Railroad (Wikipedia)
Two open-air amphitheaters for concerts and festivals (Wikipedia)
Transcending, a monument dedicated to the labor movement (Wikipedia)
Detroit-Wayne County Port Authority cruise ship passenger terminal (Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: The plaza is most active during summer weekends when festivals like the Detroit Jazz Festival are held; parking is available nearby for a fee, but no payment is required on Sundays or holidays. (Visit Detroit, Pure Michigan)
attractions.nearby: Detroit Riverwalk, Renaissance Center, Monument to Joe Louis (The Fist), Spirit of Detroit Statue (Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Rivertown (also known as the Rivertown-Warehouse District) (Rivertown Detroit Association, WXYZ 7 News Detroit)
attractions.description: Rivertown is a scenic waterfront neighborhood and historic warehouse district located along the Detroit River, extending from the Renaissance Center to the MacArthur Bridge. Once an industrial hub, it has been revitalized into a trendy urban community featuring upscale lofts, riverfront parks, and a vibrant mix of dining and entertainment options. (Visit Detroit, Gensler)
attractions.address: 2711 E. Jefferson Avenue, Detroit, MI 48207 (Rivertown Detroit Association)
attractions.region: Downtown Detroit / East Jefferson Corridor (Apartments.com, Homes.com)
attractions.hours: Daily: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM (Public areas and Riverfront parks) (Tripadvisor, Detroit Riverfront Conservancy)
attractions.admission: Free (Association membership and public access to parks) (Rivertown Detroit Association, Detroit Riverfront Conservancy)
highlights: Scenic views along the Detroit Riverwalk, including the William G. Milliken State Park and Harbor (Apartments.com)
The Aretha Franklin Amphitheatre, a premier outdoor music venue with a 6,000-seat capacity (City of Detroit)
The Dequindre Cut Greenway, a popular rail-to-trail conversion connecting the riverfront to Eastern Market (The Jaxson)
Historic architecture featuring late 19th-century warehouses repurposed into residential lofts and studios (YouTube)
Atwater Brewery, a staple of the local craft beer scene located within the district (Reddit)
Access to Robert C. Valade Park, featuring a sandy beach, children's play areas, and seasonal events (YouTube)
attractions.tips: The area is highly bikeable and features some of the city's best-protected bike lanes; use the Dequindre Cut for a traffic-free connection to Eastern Market. Visit during sunset for spectacular views of the Detroit skyline and across the river to Windsor, Ontario. (Reddit, Logical Property Management)
attractions.nearby: Renaissance Center, Belle Isle Park, Eastern Market, Greektown, and Hart Plaza (United Van Lines, Apartments.com)

attractions.name: Boston-Edison Historic District
attractions.description: The Boston-Edison Historic District is a premier residential neighborhood in the heart of Detroit, featuring over 900 homes built primarily between 1905 and 1925. It is renowned for its diverse architectural styles and as the former home of icons like Henry Ford and Berry Gordy.
attractions.address: PO Box 02100 (Bounded by W. Boston Blvd, Woodward Ave, Edison Ave, and Linwood Ave)
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (Residential area; daylight visits recommended)
attractions.admission: Free to visit neighborhood; Holiday Home Tour tickets approx. $50 (based on 2025 event)
highlights: Over 900 historically significant homes spanning 36 city blocks.
Diverse architectural styles including Tudor Revival, Italian Renaissance, and Prairie Style.
Former residence of Henry Ford, Berry Gordy, and Joe Louis.
Annual Holiday Home Tour featuring internally decorated mansions.
Self-guided walking and driving tours available via the official website.
Designated a Michigan State Historic Site and listed on the National Register of Historic Places.
attractions.tips: Visit during daylight hours for the best views of the architecture; download the self-guided tour PDFs from the official website before visiting. Voigt Park is a great central spot for a break.
attractions.nearby: Motown Museum, Fisher Building, Detroit Institute of Arts, Wayne State University, Cathedral of the Most Blessed Sacrament, Voigt Park.

attractions.name: Charles H. Wright Museum of African American History (The Wright)
attractions.description: The Charles H. Wright Museum of African American History opens minds and changes lives through the exploration and celebration of African American history and culture. For over half a century, it has dedicated itself to documenting the African American experience, housing a collection of over 35,000 artifacts. Its state-of-the-art 125,000 square-foot facility in Midtown Detroit serves as a space for education, celebration, and reflection (The Wright).
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday & Wednesday: 9 AM - 5 PM; Thursday: 9 AM - 7 PM; Friday, Saturday, & Sunday: 9 AM - 5 PM (The Wright).
attractions.admission: Adults (18-61): $15, Seniors (62+): $12, Youth (6-17): $12, Children (5 & under): Free, Members: Free. Premiere Exhibitions add $10 (The Wright).
highlights: 'And Still We Rise': A 22,000-square-foot core exhibit surveying African American history (The Wright).
'Ring of Genealogy': A significant circular floor installation in the Ford Freedom Rotunda (The Wright).
'Stories in Stained Glass': Narrative glass works detailing African American history (The Wright).
'Luminosity: A Detroit Arts Gathering': A 60th-anniversary exhibition featuring Detroit artists (The Wright).
Extensive Collection: Over 35,000 historical artifacts and archival materials (The Wright).
attractions.tips: Allow at least 2 hours to see all exhibitions. Bank of America cardholders receive one free admission ticket on the first full weekend of each month. Secure parking is available behind the museum for a flat rate of $7 (The Wright).
attractions.nearby: Michigan Science Center, Detroit Institute of Arts (The Wright)

attractions.name: Detroit Historical Museum Detroit Historical Society
attractions.description: The Detroit Historical Museum brings Detroit’s vibrant history to life with interactive exhibits and engaging programs. It chronicles the life and times of the Detroit region, preserving and sharing its rich history from its 18th-century origins to its status as the world's automotive capital. Detroit Historical Society, Visit Detroit
attractions.address: 5401 Woodward Ave, Detroit, MI 48202 Detroit Historical Society
attractions.region: Midtown / Cultural Center Historic District Detroit Historical Museum - Wikipedia, Tripadvisor
attractions.hours: Wednesday - Saturday: 10am - 5pm, Sunday: 1pm - 5pm. Detroit Historical Society

Family Discount: $1 off per ticket for households with 5+ paid admissions. Detroit Historical Society

America’s Motor City: Explore the history of the auto industry in Detroit. Detroit Historical Society
The Glancy Trains: A massive collection of model trains and toys. Detroit Historical Museum - Wikipedia
Detroit 67: Perspectives: An exhibit focused on the 1967 Detroit rebellion. Visit Detroit

attractions.name: Detroit Institute of Arts Detroit Institute of Arts
attractions.description: The Detroit Institute of Arts houses one of the largest and most significant art collections in the United States, featuring over 65,000 works that span from ancient civilizations to the present. The museum is renowned for its diverse galleries, including the world-famous Detroit Industry Murals by Diego Rivera and an extensive collection of American and European masterpieces. Tripadvisor, Detroit Historical Society

attractions.region: Midtown / Cultural Center, Detroit Furnished Rentals

attractions.name: Detroit Public Library Main Branch
attractions.description: The Detroit Public Library Main Branch serves as the headquarters of the city's library system and is a masterpiece of Italian Renaissance architecture designed by Cass Gilbert. Constructed with Vermont marble, the building features grand halls, intricate murals, and houses several world-class special collections. It is a contributing property to the Cultural Center Historic District and has been a cornerstone of Detroit's intellectual and cultural life since opening in 1921.
attractions.hours: Mon: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Tue: 12:00 PM – 8:00 PM, Wed: 12:00 PM – 8:00 PM, Thu: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Fri: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sat: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sun: 1:00 PM – 5:00 PM (Closed Sundays from May 22 through Labor Day) (Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Sunday (Closed Sundays during summer) (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Official Website)
highlights: Burton Historical Collection, featuring rare manuscripts and records of Detroit and Michigan history (Wikipedia)
Ernie Harwell Sports Collection, a premier archive of baseball and Detroit Tigers history (Wikipedia)
Adam Strohm Hall, showcasing the stunning 'Man's Mobility' triptych mural by John Coppin (Model D Media)
Pewabic Pottery fireplace in the HYPE Teen Center, decorated with tiles depicting classic fairy tales (Wikipedia)
Art and Architecture tours available to the public to explore the building's historic design (Official Website)
attractions.tips: Visit the third floor to see the most impressive architectural details and murals, including the Adam Strohm Hall. Public art and architecture tours are typically held on the third Sunday of the month from October through May. Metered street parking and nearby parking structures are available, though they can be busy due to proximity to the DIA. (Model D Media, Yelp)
attractions.nearby: Detroit Institute of Arts (DIA), Detroit Historical Museum, Wayne State University, Michigan Science Center, Charles H. Wright Museum of African American History (Official Website, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Detroit Symphony Orchestra (DSO) DSO
attractions.description: The Detroit Symphony Orchestra (DSO) is America’s fourth-oldest orchestra and a cornerstone of the city's cultural heritage. Known for its artistic excellence and world-class performances, it resides in the historic Orchestra Hall, celebrated as one of the most acoustically perfect concert halls in the world Wikipedia, Visit Detroit
attractions.hours: Box Office: Monday–Friday 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; plus 2 hours prior to most concerts. Performance times vary DSO Contact
attractions.days_open: Monday–Friday (Box Office); performance days vary (typically Friday–Sunday and some weekdays) DSO Guide
attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by performance and seating tier; typically ranging from $21.00 to $110.25+ for classical series. Special promotions like $3.13 tickets are occasionally offered on Detroit's 313 Day DSO Tickets, Visit Detroit
highlights: Founded in 1887, making it the fourth-oldest symphony orchestra in the United States Visit Detroit
Based in the historic Orchestra Hall, a Beaux-Arts landmark renowned for its superior acoustic properties Wikipedia)
Part of the Max M. and Marjorie S. Fisher Music Center, which includes 'The Cube' for modern, curated experiences DSO
Home to the 'Classical Roots' program, which has honored African American composers and musicians since 1978 Visit Detroit
Became the first orchestra in the world to broadcast a symphonic concert on the radio in 1922 NPS
attractions.tips: Use e-tickets on your mobile device for faster entry. Self-parking is available at the DSO Parking Deck (81 Parsons St) for a standard fee of $15. Arrive 30-45 minutes early for security screening, and note the strict bag policy (max 14"x14"x6"). Visit the Paradise Lounge on the second floor for pre-concert food and drinks DSO Experience Guide
attractions.nearby: Majestic Theatre, Garden Bowl, Selden Standard, The Block, Detroit Institute of Arts (DIA), and Michigan Science Center Tripadvisor, NPS

attractions.name: Founders Brewing Co. Detroit Taproom Wikipedia
attractions.description: Founders Brewing Detroit was a 14,000-square-foot brewery, restaurant, and taproom located in the Midtown/Cass Corridor neighborhood. Opened in late 2017, it served as the company's first-ever taproom outside its Grand Rapids headquarters, featuring Detroit-inspired food, taproom exclusives, and a four-barrel brewing facility. The location was known for its industrial feel, retractable garage doors, and outdoor biergarten, but it permanently closed in May 2023. Wikipedia, Visit Detroit

attractions.name: Little Caesars Arena
attractions.description: Little Caesars Arena is a premier multi-purpose arena in Midtown Detroit, serving as the home for the NHL's Detroit Red Wings and the NBA's Detroit Pistons (Wikipedia). Opened in 2017, it features a unique 'deconstructed' design with a glass-roofed concourse that connects the arena to surrounding offices and shops (HOK).

attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by event, typically starting at around $10 with average prices near $80 (Vivid Seats). Specific event pricing can be found on Ticketmaster.

Unique 'deconstructed' layout with a year-round indoor concourse (HOK)
State-of-the-art 5,100-square-foot centerhung scoreboard (Wikipedia)
Part of The District Detroit, a massive sports and entertainment development (313 Presents)

Arrive at least 60-90 minutes early for security screening and to explore the unique concourse (Vivid Seats). Use the QLINE streetcar for convenient transport to and from the arena (Reddit).

attractions.description: The Michigan Science Center (MiSci) is a premier Smithsonian-affiliated science museum located in Detroit's Cultural Center. It features over 220 interactive exhibits, live stage shows, and multiple theaters designed to inspire curious minds of all ages through hands-on STEM exploration. Wikipedia, Michigan Science Center

attractions.description: Midtown Detroit is the city's premier cultural and educational hub, situated along Woodward Avenue just north of Downtown. This vibrant district is home to world-class institutions like the Detroit Institute of Arts, Wayne State University, and the Detroit Medical Center. It features a diverse mix of historic architecture, modern lofts, and a thriving scene of independent restaurants, bars, and boutiques. Midtown Detroit Inc, Wikipedia

attractions.name: New Center (Detroit)
attractions.description: New Center is a historic commercial and residential district in Detroit, originally developed in the 1920s as a secondary business hub to the downtown area. It is renowned for its architectural landmarks, including the Art Deco Fisher Building and Cadillac Place, and serves as a vibrant cultural enclave and residential community north of Midtown.
attractions.hours: District: Open 24/7; New Center Park (seasonal): Wednesday–Saturday 8:00 AM – 9:00 PM (typical during summer event season)
attractions.days_open: Open year-round; Park events typically Wednesday–Saturday (June–August)
attractions.admission: Free (admission to neighborhood and New Center Park events)
highlights: Home to the Fisher Building, a National Historic Landmark known as 'Detroit's largest art object.'
Site of Cadillac Place, the former world headquarters of General Motors and now a major state office complex.
Features New Center Park, a unique green space hosting free summer movies, concerts, and family events.
Proximity to the Motown Museum, located just west of the neighborhood boundaries.
Host to the annual 'Detroit Auto Scene' news and historically significant retail corridors.
Anchored by major institutions like Henry Ford Hospital and the College for Creative Studies.
attractions.tips: Visit the Fisher Building lobby to admire its stunning Art Deco mosaics and architecture. Check the New Center Park schedule for free outdoor movie nights on Wednesdays and Fridays during the summer months. The QLine streetcar provides easy transit access from Downtown and Midtown to the New Center station.

attractions.name: Orchestra Hall
attractions.description: Renowned for its superior acoustics, Orchestra Hall is a historic 2,000-seat venue that serves as the home of the Detroit Symphony Orchestra. Designed by architect C. Howard Crane and opened in 1919, it is listed on the National Register of Historic Places and remains one of America's most acoustically perfect concert halls (Wikipedia), Detroit Symphony Orchestra).
attractions.hours: Box Office: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; plus 2 hours prior to all performances (Detroit Symphony Orchestra).
attractions.days_open: Monday–Friday (Box Office); Event days vary (Detroit Symphony Orchestra).
attractions.admission: Tickets typically start at $31, with average prices around $56. Prices range from $19 to $255+ depending on the performance; discounts are available for students, children (ages 3-12), and seniors. Some community events and outdoor concerts are free (Vivid Seats, ClickOnDetroit).
highlights: Renowned for near-perfect acoustics, widely considered one of the best in the world.
Home to the Detroit Symphony Orchestra, the fourth oldest orchestra in the United States.
Historic 1919 architecture designed by the noted theater architect C. Howard Crane.
Listed on the National Register of Historic Places since 1971.
Part of the Max M. and Marjorie S. Fisher Music Center cultural campus.
Formerly known as the Paradise Theater, a celebrated jazz venue from 1941 to 1951.
attractions.tips: Arrive early as accessible parking in the DSO deck fills up quickly. For drop-offs and rideshares, use the designated point on Selden Street to avoid the QLine tracks on Woodward Avenue. Pre-concert food and drinks can be enjoyed at the on-site Paradise Lounge (Detroit Symphony Orchestra, Reddit).
attractions.nearby: Detroit Historical Museum, The Block, Selden Standard, Whole Foods Market, and the QLine public transit stop (Tripadvisor, Detroit Symphony Orchestra).

attractions.name: The Masonic Temple Detroit themasonic.com

Today, it is a vibrant historic district known for its eclectic mix of Victorian architecture, trendy restaurants, and major revitalization projects like the restoration of Michigan Central Station (Visit Detroit).

attractions.name: Greenfield Village (at The Henry Ford)
attractions.description: Greenfield Village is a 80-acre outdoor living history museum established by Henry Ford in 1933. It features nearly 100 historic buildings relocated from across the U.S., including Thomas Edison’s Menlo Park lab and the Wright brothers' bicycle shop, offering immersive experiences of 300 years of American innovation and daily life.
attractions.hours: Daily: 9:30 a.m. – 5:00 p.m. (during peak season May 19 – Sept 7). Hours and days of operation vary seasonally from April through November.
attractions.days_open: Seasonal: Open Daily (May 19 – Sept 7); Tue-Sun (Apr 11 – May 18 & Sept 8 – 27); Fri-Sun (Oct 2 – Nov 29); Closed in December (except for special events like Holiday Nights).

The Wright Brothers' Home and Bicycle Shop
Working Farms district with historic livestock and crops
Liberty Craftworks showcasing glassblowing and pottery artisans
Model T rides around the historic village streets
The Weiser Railroad featuring steam locomotives and a 19th-century roundhouse
attractions.tips: Buy tickets online in advance to save on admission. Consider purchasing a Ride Pass for unlimited access to the Model T, train, and carousel. Plan for a full day as the village covers 80 acres with seven distinct historic districts. Wear comfortable walking shoes.

attractions.name: City of Hamtramck
attractions.description: Hamtramck is a uniquely diverse and densely populated city enclave within Detroit, known as "The World in Two Square Miles." It features a rich cultural fusion reflecting its history as a Polish immigrant center and its modern status as the only Muslim-majority city in the United States (Wikipedia, Hamtramck City Website).
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Saturday - Sunday: Closed (Hamtramck City Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Tripadvisor)
highlights: Pączki Day: An annual celebration on Fat Tuesday with world-famous Polish donuts. (Facebook)
Hamtramck Disneyland: A renowned folk art installation created by Dmytro Szylak. (Tripadvisor)
Polish Art Center: A local institution promoting and preserving Polish heritage through artifacts and workshops. (Wikipedia)
St. Florian Catholic Church: A historic neo-Gothic architectural masterpiece designed by Ralph Adams Cram. (Facebook)
Labor Day Festival: A massive annual event featuring parades, food tents, and live music. (Hamtramck City Website)
Ukrainian American Archives & Museum: Dedicated to preserving Ukrainian folk art and history. (Hamtramck City Website)
attractions.tips: Hamtramck is exceptionally walkable due to its compact "two square mile" size. Visitors should try the smash burgers at Slap Box on Conant and explore the vibrant music scene at local independent venues. (Wikipedia, Facebook)

attractions.name: Cranbrook Art Museum
attractions.description: Cranbrook Art Museum is a leading contemporary art museum located at the heart of the Cranbrook Educational Community in Bloomfield Hills, Michigan. Designed by renowned architect Eliel Saarinen and completed in 1942, it was one of the first museums in America dedicated to contemporary art. Today, it features a diverse range of changing exhibitions in art, architecture, craft, and design, along with a unique open-storage Collections Wing accessible to the public. Cranbrook Art Museum, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Wednesday: 11am – 5pm; Thursday: 11am – 8pm; Friday: 11am – 5pm; Saturday: 11am – 5pm; Sunday: 11am – 5pm. Closed Monday and Tuesday. Cranbrook Art Museum

highlights: Guided tours of the Collections Wing, which allows public access to the museum's storage facilities.
Saarinen House, the historic home and 'total work of art' of architect Eliel Saarinen.
The iconic Orpheus Fountain designed by sculptor Carl Milles.
A self-guided outdoor sculpture tour featuring 12 historic and contemporary works.
The museum's extensive collection of 20th and 21st-century furniture and ceramics.
attractions.tips: Visit on Thursdays for free admission and extended evening hours until 8pm. Free parking is available in designated spaces behind the museum building. Advanced online registration is recommended for certain tours and peak times. Cranbrook Art Museum

attractions.name: Cranbrook Educational Community (Cranbrook)
attractions.description: Cranbrook Educational Community is a world-renowned National Historic Landmark and center for education, science, and art. Founded in the early 20th century by George Gough Booth and Ellen Scripps Booth, the 319-acre campus features architectural masterpieces in the Arts and Crafts and Art Deco styles. It comprises a graduate Academy of Art, a contemporary Art Museum, the Institute of Science, historic House and Gardens, and independent preparatory schools (Cranbrook, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Art Museum: Wed, Fri-Sun 11:00am - 5:00pm, Thu 11:00am - 8:00pm; Institute of Science: Wed-Sat 10:00am - 5:00pm, Sun 12:00pm - 4:00pm (1st & 3rd Fri open until 10:00pm); Gardens: Daily 7:00am - 7:00pm (Summer season) (Cranbrook Art Museum, Cranbrook Institute of Science, Cranbrook House & Gardens)

highlights: Cranbrook Academy of Art: A top-ranked graduate school for art, architecture, and design (Cranbrook Academy of Art).
Cranbrook Art Museum: Features contemporary art and rotating exhibitions (Cranbrook Art Museum).
Cranbrook Institute of Science: A natural history and science museum with a planetarium and observatory (Cranbrook Institute of Science).
Cranbrook House and Gardens: The historic manor and 40 acres of manicured gardens (Cranbrook House & Gardens).
Architecture by Eliel Saarinen: The campus is a masterpiece of 20th-century design (Wikipedia).
attractions.tips: Take advantage of free admission to the Art Museum every Thursday. For the Institute of Science, visit on the first Friday of the month for free general admission courtesy of MASCO Corporation. Guided tours of the historic Cranbrook House are highly recommended but must be booked in advance as they are only available seasonally (Cranbrook Art Museum, Cranbrook Institute of Science, Cranbrook House & Gardens).

attractions.name: Detroit Zoo
attractions.description: The Detroit Zoo is one of Michigan's largest paid family attractions, situated on 125 acres and home to more than 2,000 animals representing over 200 species. Operated by the nonprofit Detroit Zoological Society, it is renowned for its award-winning, bar-less habitats and its international leadership in animal welfare, conservation, and sustainability. (Detroit Zoo, Wikipedia)

National Amphibian Conservation Center: A world-class facility dubbed 'Disneyland for toads' by the Wall Street Journal. (Detroit Zoo, Wikipedia)

attractions.description: Opened in 1904, the Belle Isle Aquarium is North America's oldest aquarium and a historic architectural marvel designed by Albert Kahn. It features a unique vaulted green glass tile ceiling and houses a diverse collection of freshwater and marine fish, including a notable collection of air-breathing fish.

attractions.description: Operated by the Detroit Zoological Society, the Belle Isle Nature Center provides nature-based programming and education on a five-acre site of forested wetlands. The center features interactive exhibits, indoor animal habitats, and a unique perspective on Michigan's native wildlife and ecosystems.

attractions.name: Belle Isle Park
attractions.description: Belle Isle Park is a scenic 982-acre island park situated in the Detroit River, offering a unique blend of natural beauty and historic cultural landmarks. As the largest city-owned island park in the United States, it features iconic attractions like the nation's oldest original aquarium, a conservatory, and a nature center, all with stunning views of the Detroit and Windsor skylines.
attractions.hours: Park: Daily 5:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Belle Isle Aquarium: Thu–Sun 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Anna Scripps Whitcomb Conservatory: Wed–Sun 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Belle Isle Nature Center: Daily 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Dossin Great Lakes Museum: Fri–Sun 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM.
attractions.admission: Free for pedestrians and cyclists. For vehicles, a Michigan Recreation Passport is required: $14 for MI residents (annual), $11 for non-residents (daily), or $40 for non-residents (annual). Admission to most island attractions, such as the Belle Isle Aquarium and Anna Scripps Whitcomb Conservatory, is free, though donations are encouraged.
highlights: Belle Isle Aquarium: The oldest original aquarium in the United States, designed by Albert Kahn.
Anna Scripps Whitcomb Conservatory: A historic 13-acre botanical garden and glass-domed conservatory.
Dossin Great Lakes Museum: Dedicated to Detroit's maritime history and Great Lakes heritage.
James Scott Memorial Fountain: A magnificent white marble fountain that is a centerpiece of the park.
Oudolf Garden Detroit: A stunning 3-acre perennial garden designed by world-renowned designer Piet Oudolf.
Belle Isle Nature Center: Features native Michigan wildlife and interactive exhibits for families.
attractions.tips: Visit during the week to avoid weekend crowds and potential bridge closures when the park reaches vehicle capacity. To save on fees, pedestrians and cyclists can enter the park for free. Be sure to check the status of specific attractions like the conservatory, which may have limited access during restoration projects.

The museum chronicles the saga of Frederick Douglass’ life as an enslaved child and young man in Baltimore, as well as the life of Isaac Myers, a free-born African American labor leader who founded the first Black-owned shipyard in the United States. attractions.description: The Guardian Building is a 43-story Art Deco masterpiece in Detroit's Financial District, famously nicknamed the "Cathedral of Finance" due to its lavish, church-like interior. Completed in 1929, the National Historic Landmark is renowned for its striking orange-brick exterior and a spectacular vaulted lobby decorated with colorful tiles and mosaics (Expedia, Wikipedia).

attractions.name: Historic Fort Wayne
attractions.description: Historic Fort Wayne is a 19th-century military star fort located on the Detroit River, originally built in 1845 to defend against potential British invasion. The site features a well-preserved masonry star fort, original limestone barracks, and an ancient Native American burial mound dating back to 1000 CE. Today, it serves as a public park and historical heritage site offering guided tours, living history re-enactments, and riverfront views.
attractions.hours: Saturday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Sunday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM (Seasonal: May through October)
attractions.admission: Free for general grounds access and parking; Guided walking tours: $8.00 per person; Some specific areas like the barracks may have a small entry fee ($5) during certain events.
highlights: The 1845 Star Fort: The oldest masonry structure of its kind in the Midwest.
Original Limestone Barracks: Restored buildings that offer a glimpse into the daily lives of soldiers.
Native American Burial Mound: A prehistoric site dating from approximately 750–1150 CE.
Commandant's House: A restored historic residence open to the public during tours.
Riverfront Views: Scenic vistas of the Detroit River and the nearby Gordie Howe International Bridge.
Living History Events: Re-enactments and special programs like Frankenfest that bring the fort's past to life.
attractions.tips: Plan to arrive by mid-morning to allow enough time to explore before the 4:00 PM closing. Guided tours are highly recommended to access interior buildings and are typically held on Saturdays at 11:00 AM and 2:00 PM. Note that most buildings are only accessible when accompanied by a guide. Wear comfortable walking shoes as the grounds are expansive and some paths may be uneven.
attractions.nearby: Gordie Howe International Bridge, Delray Boat Launch, The Landing Historic District, Ambassador Bridge, Michigan Central, West Riverfront Park.

attractions.name: James Scott Memorial Fountain
attractions.description: The James Scott Memorial Fountain is a grand Vermont white marble monument and centerpiece of Belle Isle Park, completed in 1925. Designed by architect Cass Gilbert and sculptor Herbert Adams, the fountain features a massive 510-foot wide basin adorned with 109 water outlets in the shapes of lions, turtles, and dolphins, with a central spray reaching 125 feet (Historic Detroit, Wikipedia).

attractions.tips: The fountain is currently undergoing a $6 million renovation and is scheduled to reopen in May 2027. When operational, the best time to visit is at dusk to see the light show and the backdrop of the Detroit skyline. Remember that a Recreation Passport is mandatory for vehicle entry to the island, and the statue of James Scott himself is located on the backside of the fountain facing the river (Historic Detroit, Tripadvisor).
